project.name: vex
project.summary: Foundation schema validation library family including @sylphx/vex, JSON Schema conversion, documentation website, benchmarks, CI, and release workflow wiring.
goals: Provide a fast, typed, functional schema validation library through documented package exports.
Keep core validation, JSON Schema conversion, docs, tests, benchmarks, and release wiring coherent as one library family.
Publish only consumer-neutral validation APIs and reproducible performance claims.
nonGoals: Own one product's form policy, API validation policy, data model, or error-copy strategy.
Own JSON Schema standards beyond the documented conversion behavior exported by this repository.
Publish enterprise doctrine, project manifest schema, org rulesets, rollout issue reconciliation, or shared CI and release policy.

owns.name: vex-core-package
owns.description: The @sylphx/vex package exports, validation primitives, tests, benchmarks, and documented API behavior.

owns.name: vex-json-schema-package
owns.description: The @sylphx/vex-json-schema package exports and conversion behavior for documented Vex schemas.

owns.name: vex-docs-and-release
owns.description: Docs website, workspace commands, CI workflow, benchmark evidence, and central release workflow wiring.

doesNotOwn: Consumer product schema policy, data model ownership, form UX, API error-copy strategy, or downstream validation decisions.
JSON Schema standard evolution beyond documented converter behavior and compatibility targets.
Enterprise doctrine, project manifest schema, rollout issue reconciliation, org rulesets, or shared workflow implementation.

allowedDependencies.surface: project manifest schema, standards, and rollout audit

forbiddenCouplings: Do not require consumers to import internal source paths instead of documented package exports.
Do not encode one consumer product's schema policy, error wording, or API shape as hidden Vex package behavior.
Do not publish benchmark or performance claims that lack reproducible repository-local evidence.
Do not publish enterprise process policy from this repository instead of SylphxAI/doctrine or SylphxAI/.github.

delivery.deployPath: Pull requests and merge groups run .github/workflows/ci.yml; main pushes call .github/workflows/release.yml to delegate package release handling to the central reusable workflow.
delivery.productionProof: Published package changes require CI evidence plus package/readback or consumer smoke proof for affected exports and JSON Schema conversion behavior; benchmark-sensitive changes also require reproducible benchmark evidence.

gaps.description: Upgrade from legacy CI to ADR-29 admission contexts when the package family is ready for full central policy enforcement.
Document npm/package readback, docs readback, and consumer smoke evidence required after package releases.
Turn benchmark claims and supported comparison methodology into a stable benchmark contract or generated report.
